reviews.review_description: The art, music, sound design, and story were beautiful. Sometimes the solutions to puzzles were frustrating or tedious, and I often had to crane my neck at awkward angles
Love it... This is a must have

I've played a fair few VR games on different systems at this point and this game is way up there as one of the best I have ever played. The game looks amazing and the story is great. The controls work really well. It's a real gaming show case as to what can be done in VR and what the 'Go' can do. 10/10
This is a very nice game to relax and just go through a beautifully rendered world.  Excellent experience.

reviews.review_title: Very beautiful story telling puzze game
reviews.review_description: It is a very inmersive puzze game, the story is beautiful, it makes you think trying to scape every room and scary at times, i really enjoyed it the whole  time, once you start you just want to keep going, i only had to stop to charge my oculus go,  great game!!!!! ❤️❤️❤️❤️
This game was clearly made with care and love, not only for the players, but for the characters and their environment.  Wonderful experience all around, and a joy to play.

Like immersing yourself in a meditation with puzzles that are not too difficult that you get to enjoy the scenery along the way. Great graphics, lovely music and the Mothers voice is relaxing. One of the most enjoyable games I have played.
This game Is so calming to play. Not very hard puzzles but still makes you think on what you need to do!  I recommend it and hope they make more just like it!!!
This game is cute and fun. I like the design and how you view your surroundings. I’m addicted and so is my boyfriend and he isn’t the biggest fan of VR

reviews.review_title: Nice 3D world - but too simple game
reviews.review_description: The graphics and 3D environment are quite nice. But the puzzles are too easy and repetitive. Once you got the basics you can play the game though using the same methods again and again. The story and especially the storytelling are really annoying - they should rather add some relaxing soundtrack.

Here’s a test to evaluate VR media source for credibility: if its 2019 or more recent list of the best Oculus Go adventure games does not have this at (or near) the top, the source is total crap 😉
This is a proper game, that would not be out of place on Steam today as a flat screen PC title, imho. A few things to note:
- Story is absolutely excellent, but can’t see it being enjoyed by kids, - it’s very heavy and the meaning is not spoon fed to you. Plus, it’s intertwined with an Ethiopian legend (which you can hear after you finish the game), which will make the whole thing even more confusing to some.
- Controls are spot on, imho.
- Level design is way above what you’d expect from a mobile game.
- Puzzles are easy and mostly observation based. The game is mostly driven by the story, imho.
- Some tense moments, but nothing too stressing.
- There are five long chapters, if I remember correctly.
- Graphics are great and the sense of scale, especially from Chapter 2 on, is great. Also, the level of detail is highly impressive. Note that the trailer shows a flat screen version of the game, not the VR one, it is not how the game looks on the Go.
That’s it. It’s a shame this dev will probably never produce a Go game again, as this one clearly flopped and they probably lost a ton of money on it (even if it’s a port from flat screen, it wasn’t done for free). 125 ratings in one year since release. That’s just sad.
Get it.

Depending on your point of view you're either a regular sized person transported into a miniature landscape or you're a giant person in a regular sized landscape...But it works !
The graphics had a really nice look to them, reminded me of baked clay or terracotta which gave it a certain pleasing density .
Another virtual reality experience to add to my favourites.
Very enjoyable.

A beautifully designed and imaginative narrative game everyone should have on their Go. Game play is simple and straightforward, though I did get stuck once because I was not systematic enough in my approach. (Many thanks to the Devs who set me right with an almost instant reply to my email.) This is perhaps more interactive storytelling than game so I would recommend taking it slowly to explore and enjoy the VR environments in each section. When you think about all the work and careful attention to detail that went into ECCO there is no doubt it deserves five stars.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your great comment!

reviews.review_description: I rarely write reviews but this game was so well done, I felt compelled to write this review. It seemed almost like a play. It was fun to play and the ending was so bittersweet. I loved it.

This is a beautifully designed game. The world, the story, the visuals, the music, and the voice acting, all come together in a coherent experience that is highly immersive. The puzzles are on the easier side but provide hours of game play. The story is moving and had me in tears, occasionally. If other developers take even a little inspiration from this games, we have a bright future ahead of us.

I really enjoyed playing EQQO. It has a good story and decent puzzles that get harder as the game goes on. At times it is hard to see important clues from the angles provided. In fact I am stuck on what I believe is the last puzzle where I have to complete some action with a skeleton arm, but trying to move it to various areas is proving impossible, and either I am missing something obvious or it's a game flaw.

developer_response.body:
thanks a lot for your kind words about Eqqo!
As for the puzzle, you have to grab the arm and move it to one of the slab (and go with Eqqo on the second one!)

reviews.review_description: Enjoyable game
Beautiful art style well voiced and great controls and tons of content all for under 5$
Reminds me of Moss on PSVR. Beautiful. One grip, I wish there was a smooth turning option to complement the snap turning. Ended up playing standing to look around.
Great story based game. Unique style and graphics. Loved every minute of playing this game.
